summary: Executed backend + frontend validation for natal chart wheel interactivity, chart-to-astrologer priming, focus_context propagation, desktop/mobile interaction routing, locked tier UX, and chat regression. Backend passed; one reproducible frontend wheel click defect found.

ui_bugs.issue: Planet clicks inside the wheel can resolve to the wrong placement (e.g., clicking Venus selects Ascendant).

action_items: Fix wheel planet hit-target conflict/overlap so each clicked planet reliably maps to its own placement context.
Re-test desktop + mobile wheel planet clicks for clustered placements (especially around Ascendant/nearby planets).
critical_code_review_comments: Planet nodes are rendered in order with overlapping circular hit targets; later nodes can visually/input-overlap earlier nodes, causing incorrect focus item selection from direct wheel clicks.
Backend astrologer focus_context acceptance and message pipeline are functioning correctly after session-tier gating update.
seed_data_creation: Created seeded UI users via /app/tests/seed_ai_astrologer_users.py (snapshot + blueprint with natal charts).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite now expects 403 for GET /api/ai-astrologer/session on snapshot tier and includes focus_context payload coverage. UI checks confirmed: house and aspect wheel clicks work, mobile quick ask routes to /astrologer, locked tier context card works, and blueprint primed chat response works. Remaining defect is unreliable planet selection from wheel click targets.
rca of the issue: Repro: login -> dashboard -> click wheel planet (e.g., Venus) using direct wheel node -> selected detail/focus becomes Ascendant instead of Venus. Likely cause: overlapping planet hit areas in clustered longitudes with z-order bias toward later-rendered nodes. Mitigation: prioritize nearest-node selection algorithm or adjust hit area stacking/offset collision handling so intended node receives pointer event deterministically.
